query: Show me new vs repeat customers by month for the last 6 months.
articleText:
AGENT RESPONSE

07:24 PM

medium confidence

Over the last six months (July–December 2025), new customers averaged 1.19M per month while repeat customers averaged 1.57M per month. New customers ranged from 1.16M (lowest) to 1.24M (highest), and repeat customers ranged from 1.53M to 1.63M across the period.

WHY IT MATTERS

Repeat customers consistently outnumber new customers by approximately 32%, indicating a strong retention base that drives ongoing transaction volume.

Priority Insights

high importance

Stable Repeat Customer Base Dominates Volume

Repeat customers consistently represent 57% of total monthly customer activity, with new customers at 43%, showing a mature customer base.

medium importance

Both Segments Show Modest Growth Range

New customers grew from 1.16M to 1.24M (6.5% range) while repeat customers grew from 1.53M to 1.63M (6.4% range) across the six-month window.

medium importance

December Marks Peak for Both Segments

Both new customers (1.24M) and repeat customers (1.63M) reached their six-month highs in December 2025.

ASSUMPTIONS

Interpretation constraints used for this answer.

1
Customer classification as 'new' or 'repeat' is determined by transaction history within the dataset's time scope; definitions may vary by business logic.
2
Monthly figures represent distinct customer counts, not cumulative totals across months.
3
The six-month period (July–December 2025) reflects the most recent complete months available in the dataset.
4
Repeat-to-new ratio is calculated as the average repeat customers divided by average new customers across the period.
